# TranscriptGrab

## About
TranscriptGrab is a free, open-source web application that extracts full transcripts
from YouTube videos. Users paste a YouTube URL and receive a clean, searchable transcript
with options to export as TXT, SRT, or PDF. No account or installation required.

## URL
https://youtube-transcript-tool.onrender.com/

## Features
- Instant YouTube transcript extraction (under 3 seconds)
- Side-by-side video player synced with transcript in real time
- Real-time search within transcript with keyword highlighting
- Export as TXT plain text file
- Export as SRT subtitle file (compatible with video editors)
- Export as PDF document
- Multi-language transcript support (50+ languages)
- Dark and light theme toggle
- No signup or account required
- No advertisements
- 100% free
- Open source: https://github.com/Talha4565/Youtube-Transcript-Tool

## Use Cases
- Students extracting lecture notes from YouTube videos
- Content creators researching and repurposing video content
- Researchers quoting from video interviews
- Journalists referencing video sources accurately
- Developers needing video text data
- Anyone who prefers reading over watching

## Comparison
Unlike YouTube's built-in transcript feature, TranscriptGrab offers:
- Searchable text with highlighting
- Multiple export formats (TXT, SRT, PDF)
- Side-by-side video synchronization

Unlike Tactiq or other browser extensions, TranscriptGrab:
- Requires no installation or account creation
- Works directly in the browser
- Is completely free with no premium tier

## Technical Details
- Built with Python 3.11 and Flask
- Uses youtube-transcript-api for transcript extraction
- ReportLab for PDF generation
- Vanilla HTML/CSS/JavaScript frontend
- Deployed on Render.com
- Open source under MIT license

## Creator
Talha — https://github.com/Talha4565

## Keywords
youtube transcript, youtube to text, video transcript extractor, subtitle downloader,
SRT export, free transcript tool, no signup transcript, open source youtube tool
